_attribution: LA FORJA team (Manuel Peña Zúñiga). Original item authored by the team.
stem: From an urn holding 3 red balls and 2 blue balls, two balls are drawn one after the other, without replacement. What is the probability of getting a red ball and a blue ball?
author_rationale: The author had both possible orders in mind: P(red then blue) + P(blue then red) = (3/5)(2/4) + (2/5)(3/4) = 6/20 + 6/20 = 12/20 = 3/5.

intended_defect.description: The stem never states whether the order of the draws matters. 'Getting a red ball and a blue ball' supports both the unordered reading (either of the two sequences) and the ordered reading implied by the sequential wording 'one after the other', where the red ball is the first one drawn.
intended_defect.expected_finding: Reading A = order does not matter → (3/5)(2/4) + (2/5)(3/4) = 3/5. Reading B = red first, then blue → (3/5)(2/4) = 3/10. The answers differ, so the ambiguity attack is valid.
